diagrams.title: Feature Scaling Methods Comparison
diagrams.description: Shows three scaling techniques applied to the same dataset with outliers: Min-Max (distorted by outliers), Standardization (slightly affected), Robust Scaling (unaffected)
diagrams.purpose: Understand when each scaling method is appropriate based on data characteristics

diagrams.title: Feature Selection Methods Taxonomy
diagrams.description: Hierarchical diagram showing Filter (Chi-square, Correlation, Mutual Information, Variance Threshold), Wrapper (RFE, Forward, Backward), and Embedded (LASSO, Tree Importance) methods
diagrams.purpose: Visualize different approaches and their relationships for selecting appropriate method

diagrams.title: RFE Algorithm Flowchart
diagrams.description: Step-by-step flowchart of Recursive Feature Elimination: Start with all features → Train model → Rank features → Remove weakest → Check if desired features reached → Repeat or End
diagrams.purpose: Understand iterative nature of wrapper methods and implementation logic

diagrams.title: Scaling Impact on KNN Decision Boundary
diagrams.description: Comparison showing how unscaled features create biased decision boundaries versus properly scaled features creating accurate boundaries in KNN
diagrams.purpose: Visualize why scaling is critical for distance-based algorithms
